subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the mechanism of nerve impulse transmission across a synapse
Differentiate between voluntary, involuntary, and reflex actions
Describe the localized functions of the Forebrain, Midbrain, and Hindbrain
Analyze the role of plant hormones (Auxins, Gibberellins, Cytokinins, and Abscisic acid) in growth and inhibition
Explain the feedback mechanism of hormonal secretion in humans
Correlate the chemical and nervous coordination systems in animals

lesson_plan.title: Nerve Impulse and Synaptic Transmission
topics_covered: Acquisition and travel of information in a neuron
teaching_strategy: Step-by-step animation/drawing of chemical release at the synapse
Discussion: Why do impulses travel in only one direction?
Flowchart: Dendrite → Cell Body → Axon → Nerve Ending → Synapse
student_activities: Draw the detailed structure of a synapse with neurotransmitter vesicles
Write a 'biography' of a signal traveling from a finger touch to the spinal cord
assessment: Labeling activity: Parts of a neuron and NMJ
Short answer: Significance of the synaptic gap

lesson_plan.title: Reflex Arc and Action Classification
topics_covered: The anatomy of a Reflex Arc
Comparison: Voluntary vs. Involuntary vs. Reflex actions
teaching_strategy: Tracing the reflex pathway on a human body chart
Brainstorming: Why did reflex arcs evolve before complex thinking?
Sorting exercise: Categorizing heartbeat, walking, and hand-withdrawal
student_activities: Draw a flowchart of the reflex arc specifically for a 'hot object' stimulus
Debate: Is 'blinking' a voluntary or involuntary action?
assessment: Diagrammatic test on the Reflex Arc

lesson_plan.title: The Human Brain: Functional Mapping
teaching_strategy: Color-coding a brain map by function (e.g., Speech, Hearing, Balance)
Discussion: The role of the Medulla in controlling blood pressure and salivation
Demonstration: Attempting to write while dizzy to show Cerebellum importance
student_activities: Create a table mapping brain parts to specific daily activities
Label the protective layers (Meninges and Cranium) of the brain
assessment: Identify the 'Odd One Out' based on brain part functions
Short quiz: Functions of the Hindbrain

lesson_plan.title: Movement in Plants: Nastic and Tropic
topics_covered: Turgor pressure changes in Mimosa pudica
Types of Tropisms: Photo, Geo, Hydro, and Chemo
teaching_strategy: Time-lapse video analysis of plant growth
Explaining the mechanism of tendril movement (Thigmotropism)
Comparative chart: Nastic vs. Tropic movements
student_activities: Draw a diagram showing positive vs negative phototropism/geotropism
Observe a demonstration of roots growing toward a water source
assessment: Differentiating between 'Movement due to growth' and 'Immediate response'

lesson_plan.title: Plant Hormones (Phytohormones)
topics_covered: Mechanism of Auxin in phototropism (shady side elongation)
teaching_strategy: Diagrammatic explanation of cell elongation by Auxins
Discussion: Why do leaves fall in autumn? (Role of ABA)
Real-world application: Use of hormones in agriculture/fruit ripening
student_activities: Draw a leaf-drop or wilting plant and label the hormone responsible
Summary table: Hormone Name → Primary Function
assessment: Assertion-Reasoning on Auxin concentration and light

lesson_plan.title: Hormones in Animals & Feedback Mechanism
topics_covered: Key hormones: Thyroxine, Growth Hormone, Testosterone/Oestrogen
teaching_strategy: Feedback loop diagram on the board (Blood sugar regulation)
Discussion: Why is iodine necessary for the thyroid?
Explaining the Adrenaline 'Flight or Fight' physiological changes
student_activities: Draw a feedback loop for insulin secretion after eating a meal
List the physiological changes caused by Adrenaline in the heart and lungs
assessment: Table test: Gland, Hormone, and Deficiency disease

lesson_plan.title: Consolidation and NCERT Exemplar Review
topics_covered: Comprehensive review of nervous vs. chemical coordination
teaching_strategy: Mind-mapping the entire chapter on the board
Rapid-fire board-style MCQ round
Clarifying doubts on the difference between Pons and Medulla
student_activities: Draft a summary comparing Plant vs. Animal coordination
assessment: Review of common diagrammatic errors in neurons/brain

teaching_aids: Human Brain 3D Model and Sectional View charts
Microscope and slides of nerve tissue (if available)
teacher_tip: Students often struggle with the 'Feedback Mechanism'. Use a thermostat/AC analogy to explain how the body maintains a set point for sugar or hormones—it makes the 'biological loop' much more intuitive.
